rulings.Case Title: FARHAD ZAGHI VS. CECELIA KENNEDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Leave To File A Fourth Amended Complaint (Per Order 3/14/19)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 20, 2019, Line 10, Motion For Leave To File A Fourth Amended Complaint (Per Order 3/14/19). Defendant and Cross-Complainant Cecelia Kennedy's motion for permission to file a Fourth Amended Cross-Complaint is off calendar. By order filed March 14, 2019, the Court granted Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Farhad Zaghi's motion to strike Ms. Kennedy's Third Amended Cross-Complaint, and ordered Ms. Kennedy, within 5 days of that order, to file and serve a Fourth Amended Cross-Complaint in strict conformity with the Court's February 20, 2019 Order re Stewart Title of California Inc.'s Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. No further motion or order is required for Ms. Kennedy to file the Fourth Amended Cross-Complaint. Ms. Kennedy did not contest the Court's tentative ruling on that matter, nor did she inform the Court that she had filed an ex parte application in another department-on the same day-seeking an order shortening time for the instant motion to be heard. The Court will not entertain the instant motion, which apparently seeks leave to file further amendments and is set for hearing only five days before trial. Any further motions should be addressed to the trial judge.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KC FUNDING LLC DBA KIDS CONNECTION VS. YUANQJANG BAI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Case
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 20, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF KC FUNDING LLC DBA KIDS CONNECTION's Motion To Transfer Case. Plaintiff KC Funding, LLC dba Kids Connection's motion to change venue is denied. The venue selection clause provides that the matter should be filed in the "Central District" of the Superior Court. Plaintiff fails to show that the "Central District" is San Mateo County. The fact that the contract specified San Mateo as the place of performance is distinct from the meaning of the venue selection clause. In addition, this "wrong court" motion is untimely and plaintiff waived the venue issue. Plaintiff chose San Francisco venue when it filed this action on March 29, 2018, and defendants did not move to change venue at that time. (See People v. Simon (2001) 25 Cal.4th 1082, 1102 ["any objection that a civil action has been brought in the wrong venue must be raised by a defendant specifically and at the very outset of the proceeding or it will be considered to have been forfeited."]). Plaintiff concedes that venue in San Francisco was proper at the time of filing. Plaintiff has made no evidentiary showing that a transfer to San Mateo County will promote the convenience of witnesses and/or interests of justice, and has improperly raised the issue for the first time on reply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN Y UCHIYAMA VS. GLORIA LOPEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 20, 2019, Line 4, CROSS DEFENDANT KAREN UCHIYAMA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant Karen Y. Uchiyama's demurrer to the amended cross-complaint is overruled. The demurrer was not timely filed, was not served in accordance with CCP 1005 and 1010, and Uchiyama failed to comply with the meet and confer requirements of CCP 430.41. The demurrer to the attorney malpractice (negligence) cause of action is overruled. Uchiyama cites the wrong statute of limitations as CCP 340.6 applies to the claim. Lopez sufficiently alleges discovery of the wrongdoing to render the cross-complaint timely. (Amended Cross-Complaint, 9-11; see, e.g., Favila v. Katten Muchin Rosenman LLP (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 189, 223-225 [where it "[did] not appear clearly and affirmatively from the allegations in the complaint" that derivative claims for legal malpractice and breach of fiduciary were time-barred because of delayed discovery, "[t]his issue must be resolved on summary judgment or at trial, not on a demurrer."].) The demurrer to the fraud cause of action is overruled. Uchiyama again cites the wrong statute of limitations because CCP 338(d) governs fraud claims. As noted, Lopez's discovery allegations are sufficient and Lopez alleges reliance with the required particularity because she continued to retain Uchiyama based on the misrepresentations. (Amended Cross-Complaint, 11.). The court overrules the demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action. Even if Lopez is not entitled to restitution, she still states a claim. Finally, Uchiyama has disregarded the Court's admonition in its prior order "that a demurrer must solely be based on the words of the challenged pleading and judicially noticeable facts and must not make any arguments based on other purported facts, as this demurrer did repeatedly." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Lopez is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONALBANK VS. ANTHONY W PARAGAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Notice Of Submission On Plaintiffs Motion To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 20, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS NATIONAL BANK'S Motion To Deem Matters Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Notice Of Submission On Plaintiffs Motion To Deem Matters Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition has been filed against plaintiff's motion to deem matters admitted and for imposition of sanctions, accordingly the court will issue the following order: defendants is ordered to serve plaintiff within ten (10) days from the date of this order code compliant responses without objection to all requests for admission. If defendant fails to do so, all requests for admission will be deemed admitted and defendant will be precluded to introduce any evidence challenging the admission. Monetary sanctions against defendant in the amount of $360.00, payment of which should be made within ten (10) days from the date of this order. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare an appropriate order, submit a copy to counsel for defendant for approval as to form and deliver a copy to the court at the hearing on March 20, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: YOUNGEVITY INTERNATIONAL CORP. A CALIFORNIA VS. WAKAYA PERFECTION LLC A UTAH CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Stay Or In The Alternative To Consolidate And Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 20, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT TODD SMITH, WAKAYA PERFECTION, LLC's Motion To Stay Or In The Alternative To Consolidate And Stay. Defendants Wakaya Perfection, LLC and Todd Smith's motion to consolidate this action with case No. CGC-17-557537 is granted. The cases are consolidated for all purposes with CGC-17-557537 as the lead case. The consolidated action is stayed pending the conclusion of the federal action between the parties in the Southern Disctrict of California, Case No. 3:16-CV-00704-L-JLB (S.D.Cal. filed March 23, 2016). Currently there are three pending actions between the parties dealing in whole or in part with the issues of the presence of substances injurious to human health in defendant's products and defendant's compliance with California law as to the labelling of these products. Because the two state court actions pending before this court involve common questions of law and fact, they are consolidated for all purposes. Both actions concern defendant's alleged failure to comply with Cal. Health and Safety Code §§ 25249.5 et seq. when marketing its products allegedly containing lead and arsenic (in the first action) and acrylamide or lead (in the second action) exceeding the statutory safe harbor. The actions should be litigated as one to avoid unnecessary inefficiencies. (See In re Marriage of Davis (1977) 68 Cal.App.3d 294, 304 ["The law abhors multiplicity of actions, consuming the time of the court and entailing additional expense to the people."]; Simmons v. Superior Court (1950) 96 Cal.App.2d 119, 130 [" 'It is the policy of the law to reduce to the minimum the number of actions which may subsist between the same parties."].). Although defendant's alternative motion to consolidate the actions was not filed in both actions as required by Rule 3.350(a) of the California Rules of Court, the irregularity is harmless and only as to form because all attorneys have been served and the motion includes the caption of both cases. Plaintiff has failed to show that failing to lift the stay imposed in the lead state court case would prejudice it with respect to its claims under this action because of undue delay. This action was filed in July 2018, while the federal action has already significantly advanced to the stage of fully briefed summary judgment motions after the completion of extensive discovery and there is a significant likelihood that the conclusion of the federal action would dispose of some of the claims in both state court actions. Accordingly, extending the previously ordered stay to both state court actions is appropriate. (See, e.g., Caiafa Prof. Law Corp. v. State Farm Fire & Cas. (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 800, 803-807 [trial court did not abuse its discretion in staying state court action in favor of federal action between substantially identical parties affecting the same subject matter as the state case where the federal case had been filed nearly nine months before the state court action].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ERIK STRAND VS. FILLMORE CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 20, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT RONALD SILVA, FILLMORE CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint. Defendants Fillmore Capital Partners, LLC and Ronald E. Silva's motion for leave to file cross-complaint is granted. California's liberal policy in favor of amendment of pleadings favors granting the motion, and Plaintiff has not shown that Defendants acted in bad faith or that he would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment. Plaintiff may raise any challenge to whether the cross-complaint states a cause of action by demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
